   530	// -- Reconnaissance integration --
   531	let pendingReconTask = null;
   532	// Track multiple recon progress messages keyed by crawlerId
   533	const reconProgressMessages = new Map();
   534	
   535	/**
   536	 * Start a reconnaissance crawl from the side panel.
   537	 * Uses a lighter crawl (depth 2, max 15 pages) for speed.
   538	 */
   539	async function startReconFromSidepanel(url, originalTask) {
   540	  pendingReconTask = originalTask;
   541	  const domain = new URL(url).hostname;
   542	
   543	  addMessage('Starting reconnaissance on ' + domain + '...', 'system');
   544	
   545	  try {
   546	    const response = await chrome.runtime.sendMessage({
   547	      action: 'startExplorer',
   548	      url: url,
   549	      maxDepth: 2,
   550	      maxPages: 15,
   551	      autoSaveToMemory: true
   552	    });
   553	
   554	    if (!response || !response.success) {
   555	      addMessage('Reconnaissance failed to start: ' + (response?.error || 'Unknown error'), 'system');
   556	      pendingReconTask = null;
   557	    }
   558	  } catch (error) {
   559	    addMessage('Reconnaissance failed: ' + error.message, 'system');
   560	    pendingReconTask = null;
   561	  }
   562	}
   563	
   564	/**
   565	 * Handle progress updates from Site Explorer during reconnaissance.
   566	 * Supports multiple concurrent crawlers keyed by crawlerId.
   567	 */
   568	function handleReconProgress(data) {
   569	  const crawlerId = data.crawlerId || 'default';
   570	  const domain = data.domain || '?';
   571	
   572	  if (data.status === 'crawling') {
   573	    let progressMsg = reconProgressMessages.get(crawlerId);
   574	    if (!progressMsg) {
   575	      progressMsg = document.createElement('div');
   576	      progressMsg.id = 'recon-progress-' + crawlerId;
   577	      progressMsg.className = 'message system recon-progress';
   578	      chatMessages.appendChild(progressMsg);
   579	      reconProgressMessages.set(crawlerId, progressMsg);
   580	    }
   581	    const percent = data.maxPages > 0 ? Math.round((data.pagesCollected / data.maxPages) * 100) : 0;
   582	    progressMsg.textContent = 'Recon [' + domain + ']: ' + data.pagesCollected + '/' + data.maxPages + ' pages (' + percent + '%)';
   583	    scrollToBottom();
   584	  } else if (data.status === 'completed' || data.status === 'stopped' || data.status === 'error') {
   585	    // Remove the progress message for this crawler
   586	    const progressMsg = reconProgressMessages.get(crawlerId);
   587	    if (progressMsg) {
   588	      progressMsg.remove();
   589	      reconProgressMessages.delete(crawlerId);
   590	    }
   591	  }
   592	}
   593	
   594	/**
   595	 * Handle reconnaissance completion -- offer retry with original task.
   596	 */
   597	function handleReconComplete(data) {
   598	  // Clean up any remaining progress messages for this domain
   599	  for (const [id, el] of reconProgressMessages) {
   600	    el.remove();
   601	    reconProgressMessages.delete(id);
   602	  }
   603	
   604	  addMessage('Reconnaissance complete! Site map saved for ' + (data?.domain || 'this site') + '.', 'system');
   605	
   606	  // Offer retry with the original task
   607	  if (pendingReconTask) {
   608	    const retryDiv = document.createElement('div');
   609	    retryDiv.className = 'message system new';
   610	    retryDiv.textContent = 'Site map ready. Retry your task? ';
   611	    const retryBtn = document.createElement('button');
   612	    retryBtn.className = 'retry-btn';
   613	    retryBtn.textContent = 'Retry with Site Map';
   614	    retryBtn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
   615	      // Phase 11 FINT-20 WR-03 fix -- gate the retry on the foreign-owned
   616	      // check. applyInputLockout dims chatInput/sendBtn/stopBtn/micBtn when
   617	      // the active tab is foreign-owned, but retry buttons are created
   618	      // AFTER the snapshot so they cannot be dimmed via the lockout class.
   619	      // The handleSendMessage entry already fail-closes on foreign-owned
   620	      // (defense-in-depth), but without this guard the click silently
   621	      // drops the user's intent. Early-return + console.warn surfaces the
   622	      // edge case while honoring D-11 (chip is the visible explanation).
   623	      if (await _isActiveTabForeignOwned()) {
   624	        console.warn('[sidepanel] retry blocked -- active tab is foreign-owned');
   625	        return;
   626	      }
   627	      retryDiv.remove();
   628	      chatInput.textContent = pendingReconTask;
   629	      pendingReconTask = null;
   630	      handleSendMessage();
   631	    });
   632	    retryDiv.appendChild(retryBtn);
   633	    chatMessages.appendChild(retryDiv);
   634	    scrollToBottom();
   635	  }
   636	}
   637	
   638	// Listen for explorer status and site map saved messages
   639	chrome.runtime.onMessage.addListener((message) => {
   640	  if (message.type === 'explorerStatusUpdate') {
   641	    handleReconProgress(message.data);
   642	  }
   643	  if (message.type === 'siteMapSaved') {
   644	    handleReconComplete(message.data);
   645	  }
   646	});
   647	
   648	// Keep sidepanel progress setting in sync when changed from options

   653	    showSidepanelProgressEnabled = changes.showSidepanelProgress.newValue ?? true;
   654	  }

     1	/**
     2	 * Agent Loop Engine for FSB v0.9.50
     3	 *
     4	 * Core tool_use protocol loop that replaces startAutomationLoop.
     5	 * Each iteration is a separate setTimeout callback (not a blocking while-loop)
     6	 * to survive Chrome MV3's 5-minute service worker kill.
     7	 *
     8	 * Uses Phase 135/136 modules:
     9	 *   - tool-definitions.js: TOOL_REGISTRY for tool definitions
    10	 *   - tool-use-adapter.js: format/parse for provider-specific tool_use messages
    11	 *   - tool-executor.js: executeTool for unified tool dispatch
    12	 *   - universal-provider.js: UniversalProvider for API calls
    13	 *
    14	 * Exports: runAgentLoop, runAgentIteration, buildSystemPrompt, callProviderWithTools
    15	 *
    16	 * @module agent-loop
    17	 */
    18	
    19	'use strict';
    20	
    21	// Chrome extension importScripts-compatible loading
    22	if (typeof importScripts !== 'undefined') {
    23	  // These are already loaded by background.js, but declare dependency explicitly
    24	  // in case agent-loop.js is loaded standalone in tests or other contexts
    25	  try {
    26	    if (typeof TOOL_REGISTRY === 'undefined') importScripts('ai/tool-definitions.js');
    27	    if (typeof formatToolsForProvider === 'undefined') importScripts('ai/tool-use-adapter.js');
    28	    if (typeof executeTool === 'undefined') importScripts('ai/tool-executor.js');
    29	    if (typeof UniversalProvider === 'undefined') importScripts('ai/universal-provider.js');
    30	  } catch (_e) {
    31	    // Already loaded -- ignore
    32	  }
    33	  // Phase 156-158 modules

    35	    if (typeof CostTracker === 'undefined') importScripts('ai/cost-tracker.js');
    36	    if (typeof TranscriptStore === 'undefined') importScripts('ai/transcript-store.js');
    37	    if (typeof HookPipeline === 'undefined') importScripts('ai/hook-pipeline.js');

   125	
   126	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   127	// Phase 233: Meta-cognitive attempt tracking.
   128	//
   129	// Source-of-truth-independent loop detection: counts mutation tool attempts
   130	// directly from the LLM's per-iteration toolCalls (not from session.actionHistory,
   131	// which has known recording gaps). Threshold: warn at 4, force-stop at 6
   132	// attempts on the same target within a 12-iteration sliding window.
   133	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   134	var _META_INTERACT_TOOLS = {
   135	  click: 1, click_at: 1, double_click: 1, double_click_at: 1,
   136	  type_text: 1, press_enter: 1, press_key: 1, clear_input: 1,
   137	  select_option: 1, check_box: 1, hover: 1, focus: 1,
   138	  drag_drop: 1, drag: 1, click_and_hold: 1, drag_variable_speed: 1,
   139	  execute_js: 1, fill_credential: 1
   140	};
   141	var _META_WARN_THRESHOLD = 4;
   142	var _META_FORCE_STOP_THRESHOLD = 6;
   143	var _META_WINDOW_ITERATIONS = 12;
   144	
   145	function _extractMetaTargetKey(call) {
   146	  if (!call || !call.args) return null;
   147	  var a = call.args;
   148	  if (a.selector) return 'sel:' + String(a.selector).slice(0, 80);
   149	  if (a.elementId) return 'id:' + String(a.elementId).slice(0, 40);
   150	  if (a.element_id) return 'id:' + String(a.element_id).slice(0, 40);

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * Progress hook handlers for broadcasting lifecycle events through
     3	 * the SessionStateEmitter.
     4	 *
     5	 * Four focused factory functions, each returning a handler for a specific
     6	 * lifecycle event:
     7	 *   - createToolProgressHook      -> afterToolExecution
     8	 *   - createIterationProgressHook -> afterIteration
     9	 *   - createCompletionProgressHook -> onCompletion
    10	 *   - createErrorProgressHook     -> onError
    11	 *
    12	 * Phase 159 registers these on the HookPipeline.  Phase 158 creates them
    13	 * as standalone factories only -- it does NOT replace the existing
    14	 * sendStatus calls in agent-loop.js (D-04).
    15	 *
    16	 * All handlers always return { shouldStop: false } -- progress hooks
    17	 * never halt automation (D-05).  Each is wrapped in try/catch so a
    18	 * buggy progress hook cannot kill the pipeline.
    19	 *
    20	 * @module hooks/progress-hook
    21	 */
    22	
    23	'use strict';
    24	
    25	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    26	// STATE_EVENTS values (inline to avoid importing state-emitter.js)
    27	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    28	
    29	var TOOL_EXECUTED = 'tool_executed';
    30	var ITERATION_COMPLETE = 'iteration_complete';
    31	var SESSION_ENDED = 'session_ended';
    32	var ERROR_OCCURRED = 'error_occurred';
    33	
    34	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    35	// createToolProgressHook
    36	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    37	
    38	/**
    39	 * Create a progress hook for afterToolExecution events.
    40	 *
    41	 * Emits STATE_EVENTS.TOOL_EXECUTED through the SessionStateEmitter.
    42	 *
    43	 * @param {Object} emitter - A SessionStateEmitter instance with emit(type, data).
    44	 * @returns {Function} Handler for pipeline.register(AFTER_TOOL_EXECUTION, handler).
    45	 */
    46	function createToolProgressHook(emitter) {
    47	  /**
    48	   * @param {Object} context - Pipeline event context.
    49	   * @param {string} context.sessionId - The session identifier.
    50	   * @param {string} context.toolName - Name of the tool that executed.
    51	   * @param {Object} context.toolResult - Result from tool execution.
    52	   * @param {number} context.iteration - Current iteration number.
    53	   * @returns {{ shouldStop: boolean }}
    54	   */
    55	  function toolProgressHandler(context) {
    56	    try {
    57	      emitter.emit(TOOL_EXECUTED, {
    58	        sessionId: context.sessionId,
    59	        toolName: context.toolName,
    60	        success: !(context.toolResult && context.toolResult.error),
    61	        iteration: context.iteration,
    62	        timestamp: Date.now()
    63	      });
    64	    } catch (err) {
    65	      console.warn('[progress-hook] toolProgressHandler error:', err);
    66	    }
    67	    return { shouldStop: false };
    68	  }
    69	
    70	  return toolProgressHandler;
    71	}
    72	
    73	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    74	// createIterationProgressHook
    75	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    76	
    77	/**
    78	 * Create a progress hook for afterIteration events.
    79	 *
    80	 * Emits STATE_EVENTS.ITERATION_COMPLETE through the SessionStateEmitter.
    81	 *
    82	 * @param {Object} emitter - A SessionStateEmitter instance with emit(type, data).
    83	 * @returns {Function} Handler for pipeline.register(AFTER_ITERATION, handler).
    84	 */
    85	function createIterationProgressHook(emitter) {
    86	  /**
    87	   * @param {Object} context - Pipeline event context.
    88	   * @param {string} context.sessionId - The session identifier.
    89	   * @param {number} context.iteration - The iteration number that completed.
    90	   * @param {number} context.totalCost - Cumulative cost so far.
    91	   * @param {number} context.inputTokens - Input tokens used in this iteration.
    92	   * @param {number} context.outputTokens - Output tokens used in this iteration.
    93	   * @returns {{ shouldStop: boolean }}
    94	   */
    95	  function iterationProgressHandler(context) {
    96	    try {
    97	      emitter.emit(ITERATION_COMPLETE, {
    98	        sessionId: context.sessionId,
    99	        iteration: context.iteration,
   100	        cost: context.totalCost,
   101	        inputTokens: context.inputTokens,
   102	        outputTokens: context.outputTokens,
   103	        timestamp: Date.now()
   104	      });
   105	    } catch (err) {
   106	      console.warn('[progress-hook] iterationProgressHandler error:', err);
   107	    }
   108	    return { shouldStop: false };
   109	  }
   110	
   111	  return iterationProgressHandler;
   112	}
   113	
   114	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   115	// createCompletionProgressHook
   116	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   117	
   118	/**
   119	 * Create a progress hook for onCompletion events.
   120	 *
   121	 * Emits STATE_EVENTS.SESSION_ENDED through the SessionStateEmitter.
   122	 *
   123	 * @param {Object} emitter - A SessionStateEmitter instance with emit(type, data).
   124	 * @returns {Function} Handler for pipeline.register(ON_COMPLETION, handler).
   125	 */
   126	function createCompletionProgressHook(emitter) {
   127	  /**
   128	   * @param {Object} context - Pipeline event context.
   129	   * @param {string} context.sessionId - The session identifier.
   130	   * @param {string} context.result - Completion result/summary.
   131	   * @param {number} context.totalCost - Total session cost.
   132	   * @param {number} context.iterations - Total iterations completed.
   133	   * @returns {{ shouldStop: boolean }}
   134	   */
   135	  function completionProgressHandler(context) {
   136	    try {
   137	      emitter.emit(SESSION_ENDED, {
   138	        sessionId: context.sessionId,
   139	        result: context.result,
   140	        totalCost: context.totalCost,
   141	        iterations: context.iterations,
   142	        timestamp: Date.now()
   143	      });
   144	    } catch (err) {
   145	      console.warn('[progress-hook] completionProgressHandler error:', err);
   146	    }
   147	    return { shouldStop: false };
   148	  }
   149	
   150	  return completionProgressHandler;
   151	}
   152	
   153	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   154	// createErrorProgressHook
   155	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   156	
   157	/**
   158	 * Create a progress hook for onError events.
   159	 *
   160	 * Emits STATE_EVENTS.ERROR_OCCURRED through the SessionStateEmitter.
   161	 *
   162	 * @param {Object} emitter - A SessionStateEmitter instance with emit(type, data).
   163	 * @returns {Function} Handler for pipeline.register(ON_ERROR, handler).
   164	 */
   165	function createErrorProgressHook(emitter) {
   166	  /**
   167	   * @param {Object} context - Pipeline event context.
   168	   * @param {string} context.sessionId - The session identifier.
   169	   * @param {Error|string} context.error - The error that occurred.
   170	   * @param {number} context.iteration - Iteration during which the error occurred.
   171	   * @returns {{ shouldStop: boolean }}
   172	   */
   173	  function errorProgressHandler(context) {
   174	    try {
   175	      emitter.emit(ERROR_OCCURRED, {
   176	        sessionId: context.sessionId,
   177	        error: (context.error && context.error.message) ? context.error.message : String(context.error),
   178	        iteration: context.iteration,
   179	        timestamp: Date.now()
   180	      });
   181	    } catch (err) {
   182	      console.warn('[progress-hook] errorProgressHandler error:', err);
   183	    }
   184	    return { shouldStop: false };
   185	  }
   186	
   187	  return errorProgressHandler;
   188	}
   189	
   190	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   191	// Exports
   192	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   193	
   194	if (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports) {
   195	  module.exports = {
   196	    createToolProgressHook: createToolProgressHook,
   197	    createIterationProgressHook: createIterationProgressHook,
   198	    createCompletionProgressHook: createCompletionProgressHook,
   199	    createErrorProgressHook: createErrorProgressHook
   200	  };
   201	}

extension/site-guides/utilities/camouflaged-close.js:290:      'Step 4: FILTER DECOY CLOSE BUTTONS -- For each candidate close button from Steps 2-3, check for decoy indicators: (a) does its onclick handler call window.open() or navigate to an external URL? codex
The normal intended flow should work if the sidepanel stays open and both `_tabRunningMap` entries survive: Tab B’s completion is persisted by `request.conversationId`, rendered only if that conversation is active, and idles the owning tab by `request.tabId`. So the failure has to be either a missing session entry at receive time, a missing delivery to the sidepanel context, or a second renderer/persistence path outside the main handler.

  1978	
  1979	/**
  1980	 * Plan 12-02 FINT-23 flush helper.
  1981	 *
  1982	 * Reads the envelope from chrome.storage.local, appends the buffered
  1983	 * messages via the sidecar's appendMessage (which enforces LRU cap = 50),
  1984	 * persists. On failure, resurrects the snapshot into the buffer so the
  1985	 * next flush retries.
  1986	 */
  1987	async function _flushMessageLog(convId) {
  1988	  var buffer = _messageLogPendingBuffer.get(convId);
  1989	  if (!buffer || buffer.length === 0) return;
  1990	  var snapshot = buffer.slice();
  1991	  buffer.length = 0;
  1992	  try {
  1993	    var bag = await chrome.storage.local.get(FSBSidepanelMessageLog.STORAGE_KEY);
  1994	    var envelope = bag[FSBSidepanelMessageLog.STORAGE_KEY];
  1995	    if (!FSBSidepanelMessageLog.isValidEnvelope(envelope)) {
  1996	      envelope = FSBSidepanelMessageLog.emptyEnvelope();
  1997	    }
  1998	    for (var i = 0; i < snapshot.length; i++) {
  1999	      FSBSidepanelMessageLog.appendMessage(envelope, convId, snapshot[i]);
  2000	    }
  2001	    var payload = {};
  2002	    payload[FSBSidepanelMessageLog.STORAGE_KEY] = envelope;
  2003	    await chrome.storage.local.set(payload);
  2004	
  2005	    // Phase 12 WR-02 fix: items appended to _messageLogPendingBuffer DURING
  2006	    // the chrome.storage.local.get + chrome.storage.local.set awaits stay
  2007	    // in the in-memory buffer (they were not part of `snapshot`). The
  2008	    // debouncer timer for this convId has already fired, so without an
  2009	    // explicit re-schedule the residual items would languish until the
  2010	    // NEXT _persistMessage call (which could be minutes). That stretches
  2011	    // the documented 200ms lost-on-crash bound (D-03) beyond contract.
  2012	    // Re-schedule another 200ms flush cycle so residuals land bounded-time
  2013	    // later, per the documented D-03 invariant.
  2014	    var residual = _messageLogPendingBuffer.get(convId);
  2015	    if (residual && residual.length > 0 && _messageLogDebouncer
  2016	        && typeof _messageLogDebouncer.schedule === 'function') {
  2017	      _messageLogDebouncer.schedule(convId, function () {
  2018	        return _flushMessageLog(convId);
  2019	      });
  2020	    }
  2021	  } catch (_e) {
  2022	    // Best-effort: failure resurrects the buffer so next flush retries.
  2023	    var current = _messageLogPendingBuffer.get(convId);
  2024	    if (current && current.length > 0) {
  2025	      _messageLogPendingBuffer.set(convId, snapshot.concat(current));
  2026	    } else {
  2027	      _messageLogPendingBuffer.set(convId, snapshot);
  2028	    }
  2029	    // Phase 12 WR-02 fix: on storage failure, also re-schedule so the
  2030	    // retry fires bounded-time later (same D-03 contract). Without this,
  2031	    // a transient storage write failure could strand the resurrected
  2032	    // buffer until the next _persistMessage call.
  2033	    if (_messageLogDebouncer
  2034	        && typeof _messageLogDebouncer.schedule === 'function') {
  2035	      _messageLogDebouncer.schedule(convId, function () {
  2036	        return _flushMessageLog(convId);
  2037	      });
  2038	    }
  2039	  }
  2040	}
  2041	
  2042	/**
  2043	 * Phase 12 FINT-23 (Plan 12-01) -- DOM-only render path for replay.
  2044	 *
  2045	 * Identical visual treatment to addMessage but bypasses addMessage entirely
  2046	 * so the future Plan 12-02 addMessage write-through hook does NOT loop a
  2047	 * hydrate replay back into chrome.storage.local (Pitfall 3 defense from
  2048	 * 12-RESEARCH Section 10).
  2049	 *
  2050	 * (role, kind) -> CSS type mapping:
  2051	 *   ('user',      'text')     -> .message.user
  2052	 *   ('assistant', 'text')     -> .message.system  (default assistant style)
  2053	 *   ('assistant', 'progress') -> .message.action  (D-12 styling reuses existing action treatment)
  2054	 *   ('assistant', 'tool')     -> .message.action  (D-12 styling)
  2055	 *   ('assistant', 'error')    -> .message.error
  2056	 *
  2057	 * No .new class. No animation setTimeout. Scrollback is not "new".
  2058	 */
  2059	function renderPersistedMessage(content, role, kind) {
  2060	  if (typeof content !== 'string' || content.length === 0) return;
  2061	  if (!chatMessages) return;
  2062	  var cssType = 'system';
  2063	  if (role === 'user') cssType = 'user';
  2064	  else if (kind === 'error') cssType = 'error';
  2065	  else if (kind === 'progress' || kind === 'tool') cssType = 'action';
  2066	  var messageDiv = document.createElement('div');
  2067	  messageDiv.className = 'message ' + cssType;
  2068	  messageDiv.textContent = content;
  2069	  chatMessages.appendChild(messageDiv);
  2070	}
  2071	
  2072	// Add message to chat with modern bubble styling
  2073	function addMessage(text, type = 'system', kind) {
  2074	  const messageDiv = document.createElement('div');
  2075	  messageDiv.className = `message ${type} new`;
  2076	
  2077	  // Handle different message types
  2078	  if (type === 'action') {
  2079	    // Format action messages nicely
  2080	    const actionText = text.replace(/Executed: (\w+)\((.*)\)/, (match, tool, params) => {
  2081	      try {
  2082	        const parsedParams = JSON.parse(params);
  2083	        const formattedParams = Object.entries(parsedParams)
  2084	          .map(([key, value]) => `${key}: "${value}"`)
  2085	          .join(', ');
  2086	        return `${tool}(${formattedParams})`;
  2087	      } catch {
  2088	        return `${tool}(${params})`;
  2089	      }
  2090	    });
  2091	    messageDiv.textContent = actionText;
  2092	  } else {
  2093	    messageDiv.textContent = text;
  2094	  }
  2095	
  2096	  // Add dismiss button for error messages
  2097	  if (type === 'error') {
  2098	    const dismissBtn = document.createElement('button');
  2099	    dismissBtn.className = 'message-dismiss';
  2100	    dismissBtn.textContent = 'X';
  2101	    dismissBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
  2102	      messageDiv.classList.add('collapsing');
  2103	      setTimeout(() => messageDiv.remove(), 300);
  2104	    });
  2105	    messageDiv.appendChild(dismissBtn);
  2106	    // Auto-collapse error after 30 seconds
  2107	    setTimeout(() => {
  2108	      if (messageDiv.parentNode && !messageDiv.classList.contains('collapsing')) {
  2109	        messageDiv.classList.add('auto-collapsed');
  2110	      }
  2111	    }, 30000);
  2112	  }
  2113	
  2114	  chatMessages.appendChild(messageDiv);
  2115	
  2116	  // Remove the 'new' class after animation
  2117	  setTimeout(() => {
  2118	    messageDiv.classList.remove('new');
  2119	  }, 400);
  2120	
  2121	  // Limit messages to prevent overflow
  2122	  while (chatMessages.children.length > 100) {
  2123	    chatMessages.removeChild(chatMessages.firstChild);
  2124	  }
  2125	
  2126	  scrollToBottom();
  2127	
  2128	  // Phase 12 FINT-23 write-through hook (Plan 12-02). Fires AFTER DOM render
  2129	  // so persistence failures never block UI. Role + kind derive from the
  2130	  // existing `type` parameter for backward compat with 60+ call sites; the
  2131	  // optional 3rd arg `kind` overrides when the caller knows the kind (e.g.
  2132	  // the Plan 12-03 autopilot listener emits kind='tool' for tool_executed).
  2133	  var _role = (type === 'user') ? 'user' : 'assistant';
  2134	  var _kind = kind;
  2135	  if (!_kind) {
  2136	    if (type === 'error') _kind = 'error';
  2137	    else if (type === 'action') _kind = 'tool';
  2138	    else _kind = 'text';
  2139	  }
  2140	  _persistMessage(_role, text, _kind);
  2141	}
  2142

  2144	function scrollToBottom() {

  2176	function normalizeAutomationOutcome(outcome, status, hasError) {
  2177	  var normalizedOutcome = typeof outcome === 'string' ? outcome.trim().toLowerCase() : '';
  2178	  if (normalizedOutcome === 'error') return 'failure';
  2179	  if (normalizedOutcome === 'success' || normalizedOutcome === 'partial' || normalizedOutcome === 'failure' || normalizedOutcome === 'stopped') {
  2180	    return normalizedOutcome;
  2181	  }
  2182	
  2183	  var normalizedStatus = typeof status === 'string' ? status.trim().toLowerCase() : '';
  2184	  if (normalizedStatus === 'partial') return 'partial';
  2185	  if (normalizedStatus === 'stopped') return 'stopped';
  2186	  if (normalizedStatus === 'error' || normalizedStatus === 'failed' || normalizedStatus === 'stuck') return 'failure';
  2187	
  2188	  return hasError ? 'failure' : 'success';
  2189	}
  2190	
  2191	function getSessionOutcomeDisplay(session) {
  2192	  session = session || {};
  2193	  var outcomeDetails = session.outcomeDetails && typeof session.outcomeDetails === 'object'
  2194	    ? session.outcomeDetails
  2195	    : {};
  2196	  var outcome = normalizeAutomationOutcome(
  2197	    session.outcome || outcomeDetails.outcome,
  2198	    session.status || outcomeDetails.outcome,
  2199	    Boolean(session.error || outcomeDetails.error)
  2200	  );
  2201	
  2202	  return {
  2203	    outcome: outcome,
  2204	    statusClass: outcome === 'success'
  2205	      ? 'completed'
  2206	      : outcome === 'partial'
  2207	        ? 'partial'
  2208	        : outcome === 'stopped'
  2209	          ? 'stopped'
  2210	          : 'error',
  2211	    statusLabel: outcome === 'success'
  2212	      ? 'completed'
  2213	      : outcome === 'partial'
  2214	        ? 'partial'
  2215	        : outcome === 'stopped'
  2216	          ? 'stopped'
  2217	          : 'failed',
  2218	    summary: outcomeDetails.summary || session.result || null,
  2219	    blocker: outcomeDetails.blocker || session.blocker || null,
  2220	    nextStep: outcomeDetails.nextStep || session.nextStep || null,
  2221	    resultText: session.completionMessage || outcomeDetails.result || session.result || outcomeDetails.summary || null,
  2222	    error: session.error || outcomeDetails.error || null
  2223	  };
  2224	}
  2225	
  2226	function removeLoginPrompt() {

  2229	    existing.remove();
  2230	  }
  2231	}
  2232	
  2233	function removePaymentPrompt() {
  2234	  const existing = document.getElementById('payment-prompt');

  2238	}

  2240	function getLatestThreadSessionRecord(sessionIndex, sessionStorage, threadHistorySessionId) {
  2241	  if (!threadHistorySessionId) return null;
  2242	
  2243	  var candidates = (sessionIndex || []).filter(function(entry) {
  2244	    var entryHistorySessionId = entry?.historySessionId || entry?.id || null;
  2245	    return entry?.id === threadHistorySessionId || entryHistorySessionId === threadHistorySessionId;
  2246	  });
  2247	
  2248	  if (candidates.length === 0) {
  2249	    return null;
  2250	  }
  2251	
  2252	  candidates.sort(function(a, b) {
  2253	    var aTime = a?.endTime || a?.startTime || 0;
  2254	    var bTime = b?.endTime || b?.startTime || 0;
  2255	    return bTime - aTime;
  2256	  });
  2257	
  2258	  var latest = candidates[0];
  2259	  return (sessionStorage && latest?.id && sessionStorage[latest.id]) || latest || null;
  2260	}

codex
I verified `createSessionHooks()` only registers the tool-progress overlay hook; it does not register the completion/iteration progress hooks, and `runAgentLoop(..., emitter: null)` confirms `session_ended` is not the live duplicate path. The live terminal path is the agent loop’s `notifySidepanel()` through `fsbBroadcastAutomationLifecycle()`.
codex
I’m going to trace the remaining strong suspects: the background’s natural completion path for repeated terminal broadcasts, and the regression test’s sandbox to see whether it sets up `_tabRunningMap`, `conversationId`, and Chrome message delivery the same way the browser does.

codex
The natural AI loop finalizes through one helper: `finalizeSession()` saves the session, calls `notifySidepanel()`, sleeps, then cleans up. The repeated `hooks.emit(ON_COMPLETION)` calls are not a UI duplicate here because the registered hook set does not include the completion hook. Next I’m looking at tests and a subtler storage interaction: live completion persist plus `fsbSessionLogs` hydrate fallback can still make duplicate visible bubbles.

  1351

    16	 *   _renderCompletionDomOnly call. It must NOT call completeStatusMessage
    17	 *   in the if-branch (that was the double-persist site).
    18	 *
    19	 *   Part 2 (D-FIX): Tab B's session completes while user is on Tab A.
    20	 *   Pre-Task-1 this dropped the entire handler body for Tab B (outer bail).

    38	 *   dropped the entire body for background-tab completions. Assertion 3.1
    39	 *   would have FAILED because the pre-fix double-persist produced 2 persist
    40	 *   calls for conv_A.

    50	const path = require('path');

    53	let passed = 0;
    54	let failed = 0;
    55	function ok(cond, msg) {

    57	  else { failed++; console.error('  FAIL:', msg); }

    63	
    64	// Brace-walking extractor copied from sidepanel-progress-tick-setter-routing.test.js.
    65	function extractAfterAnchor(src, anchor, startFrom) {
    66	  var from = (typeof startFrom === 'number' && startFrom >= 0) ? startFrom : 0;
    67	  var startIdx = src.indexOf(anchor, from);
    68	  if (startIdx === -1) return null;
    69	  var braceIdx = src.indexOf('{', startIdx);
    70	  if (braceIdx === -1) return null;
    71	  var depth = 1;
    72	  var i = braceIdx + 1;
    73	  while (i < src.length && depth > 0) {
    74	    var c = src[i];
    75	    if (c === '{') depth++;
    76	    else if (c === '}') depth--;
    77	    if (depth === 0) break;
    78	    i++;
    79	  }
    80	  if (depth !== 0) return null;
    81	  return src.slice(braceIdx + 1, i);
    82	}
    83	
    84	// =====================================================================
    85	// Part 1 -- structural sanity on the case automationComplete body
    86	// =====================================================================
    87	
    88	console.log('\nPart 1 -- post-Task-1 structural sanity:');
    89	
    90	var autoCompleteIdx = spSrc.indexOf("case 'automationComplete'");
    91	ok(autoCompleteIdx > 0,
    92	   'Part 1.1 -- case automationComplete anchor located');
    93	
    94	var autoCompleteBody = extractAfterAnchor(spSrc, "case 'automationComplete'", autoCompleteIdx);
    95	ok(autoCompleteBody !== null,
    96	   'Part 1.2 -- case automationComplete body extractable');
    97	
    98	if (!autoCompleteBody) {
    99	  console.log('\n' + passed + ' PASS / ' + failed + ' FAIL');
   100	  process.exit(failed === 0 ? 0 : 1);
   101	}
   102	
   103	ok(autoCompleteBody.indexOf('sessionKnown') !== -1,
   104	   'Part 1.3 -- relaxed outer guard variable sessionKnown present (D-FIX)');
   105	ok(autoCompleteBody.indexOf('_tabRunningMap.values()') !== -1,
   106	   'Part 1.4 -- relaxed outer guard scans _tabRunningMap for background-tab session match');
   107	ok(autoCompleteBody.indexOf('_persistMessageToConversation') !== -1,
   108	   'Part 1.5 -- _persistMessageToConversation present (unconditional persist)');
   109	ok(autoCompleteBody.indexOf('_renderCompletionDomOnly') !== -1,
   110	   'Part 1.6 -- _renderCompletionDomOnly present (DOM-only render path)');
   111	ok(autoCompleteBody.match(/completeStatusMessage\s*\(/) === null,
   112	   'Part 1.7 -- NO completeStatusMessage call in body (E-FIX: avoids double-persist via addCompletionMessage)');
   113	ok(autoCompleteBody.indexOf('isOriginatingActive') !== -1,

   115	
   116	// =====================================================================
   117	// Anchor the outer chrome.runtime.onMessage handler for sandbox eval
   118	// =====================================================================
   119	//
   120	// sidepanel.js declares THREE chrome.runtime.onMessage.addListener calls;
   121	// we need the one with the switch (request.action). Pattern copied verbatim
   122	// from sidepanel-progress-tick-setter-routing.test.js.
   123	
   124	var onMessageAnchor = 'chrome.runtime.onMessage.addListener((request, sender, sendResponse) => {';
   125	var switchAnchor = 'switch (request.action)';
   126	var switchIdx = spSrc.indexOf(switchAnchor);
   127	var bigHandlerIdx = spSrc.lastIndexOf(onMessageAnchor, switchIdx);

   129	ok(onMessageBody !== null && onMessageBody.indexOf('switch (request.action)') !== -1,
   130	   'Part 1.9 -- big chrome.runtime.onMessage handler body extractable for sandbox eval');
   131

   137	// =====================================================================
   138	// Shared sandbox harness

   140	
   141	// Build a dispatcher that compiles the listener body once and lets each
   142	// scenario inject its own per-tab map, module-scope mirrors, and stubs.
   143	
   144	function buildDispatcher(injection) {
   145	  var fnSrc = '"use strict"; ' + onMessageBody;
   146	  var fn = new Function(
   147	    'request', 'sender', 'sendResponse',
   148	    'chrome', 'console',
   149	    // Per-tab state.
   150	    '_tabRunningMap', '_activeTabIdSnapshot', '_getTabRunningEntry', '_resolveTabIdForSession',
   151	    // Module-scope mirrors.
   152	    'isRunning', 'currentSessionId', 'conversationId',
   153	    // Setters.
   154	    'setRunningState', 'setIdleState', 'setErrorState',
   155	    // DOM stubs.
   156	    'sendBtn', 'stopBtn', 'statusDot', 'statusText', 'chatInput',
   157	    'currentStatusMessage', 'currentActionGroup',
   158	    // Persistence + render stubs.
   159	    '_persistMessageToConversation', '_renderCompletionDomOnly',
   160	    'completeStatusMessage', 'addMessage', 'addCompletionMessage', 'addActionMessage',
   161	    'updateStatusMessage', 'showLoginPrompt', 'showPaymentFillConfirmation',
   162	    'loadHistoryList', 'showChatView', 'startReconFromSidepanel',
   163	    // Misc flags.
   164	    'isHistoryViewActive', 'historySessionId', 'lastRenderedTerminalSessionId',
   165	    'showSidepanelProgressEnabled',
   166	    'chatMessages', 'scrollToBottom',
   167	    // QT-uof-5 (B-FIX) -- per-tab status intent helpers. The handler's
   168	    // if-branch calls _clearTabStatusIntent after manual loader cleanup.
   169	    '_clearTabStatusIntent', '_persistTabStatusIntent', '_restoreTabStatusIntent',
   170	    fnSrc
   171	  );
   172	  return function dispatch(request) {
   173	    fn(
   174	      request, /* sender */ null, /* sendResponse */ function () {},
   175	      injection.chrome, injection.console,
   176	      injection.tabRunningMap, injection.activeTabIdSnapshot,
   177	      injection.getTabRunningEntry, injection.resolveTabIdForSession,
   178	      injection.moduleIsRunning, injection.moduleCurrentSessionId, injection.moduleConversationId,
   179	      injection.setRunningState, injection.setIdleState, injection.setErrorState,
   180	      injection.sendBtn, injection.stopBtn, injection.statusDot, injection.statusText, injection.chatInput,
   181	      injection.currentStatusMessage, injection.currentActionGroup,
   182	      injection.persistMessageToConversation, injection.renderCompletionDomOnly,
   183	      injection.completeStatusMessage, injection.addMessage, injection.addCompletionMessage, injection.addActionMessage,
   184	      injection.updateStatusMessage, injection.showLoginPrompt, injection.showPaymentFillConfirmation,
   185	      injection.loadHistoryList, injection.showChatView, injection.startReconFromSidepanel,
   186	      injection.isHistoryViewActive, injection.historySessionId, injection.lastRenderedTerminalSessionId,
   187	      injection.showSidepanelProgressEnabled,
   188	      injection.chatMessages, injection.scrollToBottom,

   190	    );
   191	  };
   192	}

   194	function makeInjection(opts) {
   195	  var tabRunningMap = new Map();
   196	  if (opts && opts.tabEntries) {
   197	    opts.tabEntries.forEach(function (e) { tabRunningMap.set(e.tabId, { isRunning: e.isRunning, sessionId: e.sessionId }); });
   198	  }
   199	  var activeTabIdSnapshot = (opts && typeof opts.activeTabId === 'number') ? opts.activeTabId : null;
   200	
   201	  // Mutable state mirrors -- closed-over by setters so tests can read post-state.
   202	  var state = {
   203	    moduleIsRunning: !!(opts && opts.moduleIsRunning),
   204	    moduleCurrentSessionId: (opts && opts.moduleCurrentSessionId) || null,
   205	    moduleConversationId: (opts && opts.moduleConversationId) || null,
   206	    sendBtnDisabled: !!(opts && opts.sendBtnDisabled),
   207	    setIdleCalls: [],
   208	    persistCalls: [],
   209	    renderCalls: [],
   210	    completeStatusCalls: [],
   211	    addCompletionCalls: [],
   212	    addMessageCalls: []
   213	  };
   214	
   215	  function getTabRunningEntry(tabId) {
   216	    if (typeof tabId !== 'number') return { isRunning: false, sessionId: null };
   217	    var e = tabRunningMap.get(tabId);
   218	    if (!e) { e = { isRunning: false, sessionId: null }; tabRunningMap.set(tabId, e); }
   219	    return e;
   220	  }
   221	  function resolveTabIdForSession(sessionId) {
   222	    if (typeof sessionId === 'string' && sessionId.length > 0) {
   223	      var iter = tabRunningMap.entries();
   224	      var nxt = iter.next();
   225	      while (!nxt.done) {
   226	        var tabId = nxt.value[0];
   227	        var entry = nxt.value[1];
   228	        if (entry && entry.sessionId === sessionId) return tabId;
   229	        nxt = iter.next();
   230	      }
   231	    }
   232	    return activeTabIdSnapshot;
   233	  }
   234	  function setIdleState(tabId) {
   235	    var t = (typeof tabId === 'number') ? tabId : activeTabIdSnapshot;
   236	    state.setIdleCalls.push({ tabId: t });
   237	    if (typeof t === 'number') {
   238	      var e = getTabRunningEntry(t);
   239	      e.isRunning = false;
   240	      e.sessionId = null;
   241	    }
   242	    if (t === activeTabIdSnapshot) {
   243	      state.moduleIsRunning = false;
   244	      state.moduleCurrentSessionId = null;
   245	      state.sendBtnDisabled = false;
   246	    }
   247	  }
   248	  function setRunningState(tabId, sessionId) {
   249	    var t = (typeof tabId === 'number') ? tabId : activeTabIdSnapshot;
   250	    if (typeof t === 'number') {
   251	      var e = getTabRunningEntry(t);
   252	      e.isRunning = true;
   253	      if (typeof sessionId === 'string' && sessionId.length > 0) e.sessionId = sessionId;
   254	    }
   255	    if (t === activeTabIdSnapshot) {
   256	      state.moduleIsRunning = true;
   257	      if (typeof sessionId === 'string' && sessionId.length > 0) state.moduleCurrentSessionId = sessionId;
   258	      state.sendBtnDisabled = true;
   259	    }
   260	  }

codex
The test is a strong clue: it does not load the full sidepanel, does not drive `chrome.runtime.sendMessage`, and injects exactly the `_tabRunningMap` entries needed for the new handler to pass. That can prove the new handler body works under ideal state, but it cannot prove the browser has the same state or that delivery reaches the same sidepanel document.

   374	// =====================================================================
   375	// Part 2 -- D-FIX scenario: Tab B completes while user is on Tab A
   376	// =====================================================================
   377	//
   378	// Setup: _tabRunningMap = { 100: {isRunning:true, sessionId:'sess_A'},
   379	//                           200: {isRunning:true, sessionId:'sess_B'} }
   380	//        _activeTabIdSnapshot = 100

   382	//        moduleConversationId = 'conv_A'
   383	//        currentStatusMessage = active loader stub
   384	// Dispatch: automationComplete for sess_B, conv_B, tabId=200, result='B done'
   385	
   386	console.log('\nPart 2 -- D-FIX: Tab B completes while user is on Tab A:');
   387	
   388	var inj2 = makeInjection({
   389	  tabEntries: [
   390	    { tabId: 100, isRunning: true, sessionId: 'sess_A' },
   391	    { tabId: 200, isRunning: true, sessionId: 'sess_B' }
   392	  ],
   393	  activeTabId: 100,
   394	  moduleIsRunning: true,
   395	  moduleCurrentSessionId: 'sess_A',
   396	  moduleConversationId: 'conv_A',
   397	  sendBtnDisabled: true,
   398	  currentStatusMessage: true,
   399	  currentActionGroup: { fake: 'group' }
   400	});
   401	
   402	var dispatch2 = buildDispatcher(inj2);
   403	try {
   404	  dispatch2({
   405	    action: 'automationComplete',
   406	    sessionId: 'sess_B',
   407	    conversationId: 'conv_B',
   408	    tabId: 200,
   409	    result: 'B done',
   410	    partial: false
   411	  });
   412	} catch (err) {
   413	  failed++;
   414	  console.error('  FAIL: Part 2 dispatch threw:', err && err.message);
   415	}
   416	
   417	// 2.1 _persistMessageToConversation called with convId='conv_B' (NOT 'conv_A')
   418	ok(inj2.state.persistCalls.length === 1 && inj2.state.persistCalls[0].convId === 'conv_B' && inj2.state.persistCalls[0].content === 'B done',
   419	   'Part 2.1 -- _persistMessageToConversation called EXACTLY ONCE with convId=conv_B, content=B done');
   420	
   421	// 2.2 _persistMessageToConversation called EXACTLY ONCE this dispatch
   422	ok(inj2.state.persistCalls.length === 1,
   423	   'Part 2.2 -- exactly one persist (D-FIX persists unconditionally; no double-persist because if-branch is NOT active)');
   424	
   425	// 2.3 Tab B's _tabRunningMap entry now has isRunning:false, sessionId:null
   426	var tabB2 = inj2.tabRunningMap.get(200);
   427	ok(tabB2 && tabB2.isRunning === false && tabB2.sessionId === null,
   428	   'Part 2.3 -- Tab B per-tab entry flipped to isRunning:false, sessionId:null (D-FIX flips background-tab entry)');
   429	
   430	// 2.4 Tab A's _tabRunningMap entry UNTOUCHED
   431	var tabA2 = inj2.tabRunningMap.get(100);
   432	ok(tabA2 && tabA2.isRunning === true && tabA2.sessionId === 'sess_A',
   433	   'Part 2.4 -- Tab A per-tab entry UNCHANGED (isRunning:true, sessionId:sess_A)');
   434	
   435	// 2.5 moduleIsRunning UNTOUCHED and moduleCurrentSessionId UNTOUCHED.
   436	//     setIdleState called with tabId=200 mutates ONLY the per-tab entry for
   437	//     tab 200; module-scope mirrors stay attached to the active tab (100).
   438	ok(inj2.state.moduleIsRunning === true,
   439	   'Part 2.5a -- moduleIsRunning still true (active-tab UI not disturbed by background completion)');
   440	ok(inj2.state.moduleCurrentSessionId === 'sess_A',
   441	   'Part 2.5b -- moduleCurrentSessionId still sess_A');
   442	
   443	// 2.6 _renderCompletionDomOnly NOT called (isOriginatingActive=false because
   444	//     originatingConvId=conv_B != moduleConversationId=conv_A)
   445	ok(inj2.state.renderCalls.length === 0,
   446	   'Part 2.6 -- _renderCompletionDomOnly NOT called (isOriginatingActive=false for background tab)');
   447	
   448	// 2.7 sendBtn stays disabled (active-tab UI undisturbed)
   449	ok(inj2.state.sendBtnDisabled === true,

   459	
   460	// 2.10 completeStatusMessage and addCompletionMessage NOT called.
   461	ok(inj2.state.completeStatusCalls.length === 0,
   462	   'Part 2.10 -- completeStatusMessage NOT called (E-FIX guarantee preserved on background path)');

   464	   'Part 2.11 -- addCompletionMessage NOT called (E-FIX guarantee preserved on background path)');
   465	
   466	// =====================================================================
   467	// Part 3 -- E-FIX scenario: Tab A completes, currentStatusMessage non-null

   470	// Setup: Tab A active, sess_A running, currentStatusMessage non-null,

   473	// E-FIX: EXACTLY ONE persist to conv_A; NO completeStatusMessage;
   474	// NO addCompletionMessage; ONE _renderCompletionDomOnly; manual remove() fires.
   475	
   476	console.log('\nPart 3 -- E-FIX: Tab A completes, currentStatusMessage non-null:');

   478	var inj3 = makeInjection({
   479	  tabEntries: [
   480	    { tabId: 100, isRunning: true, sessionId: 'sess_A' },
   481	    { tabId: 200, isRunning: true, sessionId: 'sess_B' }
   482	  ],
   483	  activeTabId: 100,
   484	  moduleIsRunning: true,
   485	  moduleCurrentSessionId: 'sess_A',
   486	  moduleConversationId: 'conv_A',
   487	  sendBtnDisabled: true,
   488	  currentStatusMessage: true,
   489	  currentActionGroup: { fake: 'group' }
   490	});
   491	
   492	var dispatch3 = buildDispatcher(inj3);
   493	try {
   494	  dispatch3({

   496	    sessionId: 'sess_A',
   497	    conversationId: 'conv_A',
   498	    tabId: 100,
   499	    result: 'A done',
   500	    partial: false
   501	  });
   502	} catch (err) {

   504	  console.error('  FAIL: Part 3 dispatch threw:', err && err.message);
   505	}
   506	
   507	// 3.1 EXACTLY ONE _persistMessageToConversation against conv_A (E-FIX: no double-persist)
   508	ok(inj3.state.persistCalls.length === 1 && inj3.state.persistCalls[0].convId === 'conv_A' && inj3.state.persistCalls[0].content === 'A done',
   509	   'Part 3.1 -- _persistMessageToConversation called EXACTLY ONCE with convId=conv_A (E-FIX: no double-persist)');
   510

   512	ok(inj3.state.completeStatusCalls.length === 0,
   513	   'Part 3.2 -- completeStatusMessage NOT called (E-FIX: if-branch bypasses double-persist site)');
   514

   516	ok(inj3.state.addCompletionCalls.length === 0,

   522

   686	    exportLogs() {

   693	
   694	    generateReport(sessionId = null) {
   695	      const relevantLogs = sessionId ? this.getSessionLogs(sessionId) : this.logs;

   697	        totalLogs: relevantLogs.length,
   698	        errors: relevantLogs.filter(l => l.level === 'error').length,
   699	        warnings: relevantLogs.filter(l => l.level === 'warn').length
   700	      };

   702	
   703	    async saveSession(sessionId, sessionData = {}) {
   704	      // Guard against invalidated extension context
   705	      if (!chrome.runtime?.id) return false;
   706	      try {
   707	        const sessionLogs = this.getSessionLogs(sessionId);
   708	        const persistedLogs = filterPersistedSessionLogs(sessionLogs);
   709	        if (sessionLogs.length === 0 && persistedLogs.length === 0) return false;
   710	
   711	        const stored = await chrome.storage.local.get(['fsbSessionLogs', 'fsbSessionIndex']);
   712	        const sessionStorage = stored.fsbSessionLogs || {};
   713	        const sessionIndex = stored.fsbSessionIndex || [];
   714	
   715	        if (sessionStorage[sessionId]) {
   716	          // APPEND MODE: Update existing session entry
   717	          const existing = sessionStorage[sessionId];
   718	          const metadata = buildPersistedSessionMetadata(sessionId, sessionData, existing);
   719	          const normalizedOutcome = normalizePersistedOutcomeFields(sessionData, existing);
   720	          // Merge logs: add only new logs (those with timestamps after existing endTime)
   721	          const newLogs = persistedLogs.filter(log => Date.parse(log.timestamp) > (existing.endTime || 0));
   722	          existing.logs = filterPersistedSessionLogs(existing.logs || []);
   723	          if (newLogs.length > 0) {
   724	            existing.logs = filterPersistedSessionLogs(existing.logs.concat(newLogs));
   725	          }
   726	          existing.endTime = Date.now();
   727	          existing.status = sessionData.status || existing.status;
   728	          existing.actionCount = sessionData.actionHistory?.length || existing.actionCount;
   729	          existing.iterationCount = sessionData.iterationCount || existing.iterationCount;
   730	          existing.conversationId = metadata.conversationId;
   731	          existing.uiSurface = metadata.uiSurface;
   732	          existing.historySessionId = metadata.historySessionId;
   733	          existing.commandCount = metadata.commandCount;
   734	          existing.commands = metadata.commands;
   735	          existing.lastTask = metadata.lastTask;
   736	          existing.lastCommandAt = metadata.lastCommandAt;
   737	          existing.totalCost = sessionData.totalCost || existing.totalCost || 0;
   738	          existing.totalInputTokens = sessionData.totalInputTokens || existing.totalInputTokens || 0;
   739	          existing.totalOutputTokens = sessionData.totalOutputTokens || existing.totalOutputTokens || 0;
   740	          existing.outcome = normalizedOutcome.outcome;
   741	          existing.outcomeDetails = normalizedOutcome.outcomeDetails;
   742	          existing.result = normalizedOutcome.result;
   743	          existing.completionMessage = normalizedOutcome.completionMessage;
   744	          existing.error = normalizedOutcome.error;
   745	          existing.blocker = normalizedOutcome.blocker;
   746	          existing.nextStep = normalizedOutcome.nextStep;
   747	          // Update task to show the latest command
   748	          if (metadata.commands.length > 1) {
   749	            existing.task = metadata.commands.map((cmd, i) => `[${i + 1}] ${cmd}`).join(' | ');
   750	          } else if (metadata.lastTask) {
   751	            existing.task = metadata.lastTask;
   752	          }
   753	          // Persist actionHistory for session replay (successful actions only, capped at 100)
   754	          if (sessionData.actionHistory) {
   755	            existing.actionHistory = (sessionData.actionHistory || [])
   756	              .filter(a => a.result?.success)
   757	              .slice(-100)
   758	              .map(a => ({ tool: a.tool, params: a.params, result: a.result, timestamp: a.timestamp }));
   759	          }
   760	          sessionStorage[sessionId] = existing;
   761	        } else {
   762	          // NEW MODE: Create session entry
   763	          const metadata = buildPersistedSessionMetadata(sessionId, sessionData);
   764	          const normalizedOutcome = normalizePersistedOutcomeFields(sessionData);
   765	          const session = {
   766	            id: sessionId,
   767	            task: metadata.commands.length > 1
   768	              ? metadata.commands.map((cmd, i) => `[${i + 1}] ${cmd}`).join(' | ')
   769	              : (metadata.lastTask || 'Unknown task'),
   770	            startTime: sessionData.startTime || Date.now(),
   771	            endTime: Date.now(),
   772	            status: sessionData.status || 'completed',
   773	            tabId: sessionData.tabId || null,
   774	            actionCount: sessionData.actionHistory?.length || 0,
   775	            iterationCount: sessionData.iterationCount || 0,
   776	            conversationId: metadata.conversationId,
   777	            uiSurface: metadata.uiSurface,
   778	            historySessionId: metadata.historySessionId,
   779	            commandCount: metadata.commandCount,
   780	            commands: metadata.commands,
   781	            lastTask: metadata.lastTask,
   782	            lastCommandAt: metadata.lastCommandAt,
   783	            totalCost: sessionData.totalCost || 0,
   784	            totalInputTokens: sessionData.totalInputTokens || 0,
   785	            totalOutputTokens: sessionData.totalOutputTokens || 0,
   786	            outcome: normalizedOutcome.outcome,
   787	            outcomeDetails: normalizedOutcome.outcomeDetails,
   788	            result: normalizedOutcome.result,
   789	            completionMessage: normalizedOutcome.completionMessage,
   790	            error: normalizedOutcome.error,
   791	            blocker: normalizedOutcome.blocker,
   792	            nextStep: normalizedOutcome.nextStep,
   793	            logs: filterPersistedSessionLogs(sessionLogs),
   794	            // Persist actionHistory for session replay (successful actions only, capped at 100)
   795	            actionHistory: (sessionData.actionHistory || [])
   796	              .filter(a => a.result?.success)
   797	              .slice(-100)
   798	              .map(a => ({ tool: a.tool, params: a.params, result: a.result, timestamp: a.timestamp }))
   799	          };
   800	          sessionStorage[sessionId] = session;
   801	        }
   802	
   803	        // Update index
   804	        const savedSession = sessionStorage[sessionId];
   805	        const snapshotCount = (this._domSnapshots && this._domSnapshots[sessionId])
   806	          ? this._domSnapshots[sessionId].length : 0;
   807	        const indexEntry = {
   808	          id: sessionId, task: savedSession.task, startTime: savedSession.startTime,
   809	          endTime: savedSession.endTime, status: savedSession.status, actionCount: savedSession.actionCount,
   810	          domSnapshotCount: snapshotCount,
   811	          totalCost: savedSession.totalCost || 0,

   813	          outcomeDetails: savedSession.outcomeDetails || null,

   828	        if (existingIndex !== -1) sessionIndex[existingIndex] = indexEntry;
   829	        else sessionIndex.unshift(indexEntry);
   830	        if (sessionIndex.length > 50) {
   831	          const toRemove = sessionIndex.slice(50);
   832	          toRemove.forEach(entry => delete sessionStorage[entry.id]);
   833	          sessionIndex.length = 50;
   834	        }
   835	        await chrome.storage.local.set({ fsbSessionLogs: sessionStorage, fsbSessionIndex: sessionIndex });

   837	        // Persist DOM snapshots to dedicated storage key
   838	        await this._persistDOMSnapshots(sessionId, sessionIndex);
   839	
   840	        console.log(`[FSB Logger] Session ${sessionId} saved with ${savedSession.logs?.length || 0} total logs, ${snapshotCount} DOM snapshots`);
   841	        return true;
   842	      } catch (error) {
   843	        if (chrome.runtime?.id) {
   844	          console.error('[FSB Logger] Failed to save session:', error);
   845	        }
   846	        return false;
   847	      }
   848	    }
   849	
   850	    async _persistDOMSnapshots(sessionId, sessionIndex) {

     1	/**
     2	 * Phase 12 FINT-23 -- pure-helper sidecar for the side panel's
     3	 * per-conversation persistent message log.
     4	 *
     5	 * Storage key: 'fsbConversationMessages' (CONTEXT D-01 + D-02).
     6	 * Envelope: { v: 1, byConv: { '<convId>': log, ... }, lru: ['<convId>', ...] }.
     7	 * Log: { v: 1, messages: [{role, content, timestamp, kind}, ...], lastWriteAt, createdAt }.
     8	 * Cap: 50 conversations; tail-eviction on 51st insert (D-04).
     9	 * Debounce: 200ms per convId; clear-and-replace (D-03).
    10	 *
    11	 * Test-seam: sidecar performs ZERO host-extension API calls. The caller
    12	 * owns envelope read/write; the debouncer accepts injected setTimeoutFn +
    13	 * clearTimeoutFn so Node tests advance simulated time deterministically.
    14	 *
    15	 * Classic-script load shape: registers on globalThis for sidepanel
    16	 * consumption AND exports for Node tests. Mirrors
    17	 * extension/ui/sidepanel-tab-conv-store.js (Phase 11 sidecar).
    18	 */
    19	(function(global) {
    20	  'use strict';
    21	
    22	  var STORAGE_KEY = 'fsbConversationMessages';
    23	  var DEFAULT_CAP = 50;
    24	  var DEFAULT_DEBOUNCE_MS = 200;
    25	  var ENVELOPE_VERSION = 1;
    26	  var ALLOWED_ROLES = { user: true, assistant: true };
    27	  var ALLOWED_KINDS = { text: true, progress: true, tool: true, error: true };
    28	
    29	  /**
    30	   * Produce a fresh empty envelope. New object every call so callers cannot
    31	   * accidentally share state through a shared reference.
    32	   * @returns {object} { v: 1, byConv: {}, lru: [] }
    33	   */
    34	  function emptyEnvelope() {
    35	    return { v: ENVELOPE_VERSION, byConv: {}, lru: [] };
    36	  }
    37	
    38	  /**
    39	   * Defensive shape check. Required at every public-API entry so corrupted
    40	   * envelopes (e.g., from DevTools-mutated storage) degrade safely.
    41	   * @param {*} env
    42	   * @returns {boolean}
    43	   */
    44	  function isValidEnvelope(env) {
    45	    if (!env || typeof env !== 'object') return false;
    46	    if (env.v !== ENVELOPE_VERSION) return false;
    47	    if (!env.byConv || typeof env.byConv !== 'object') return false;
    48	    if (!Array.isArray(env.lru)) return false;
    49	    return true;
    50	  }
    51	
    52	  /**
    53	   * Normalize a conversationId. Returns null on invalid input.
    54	   * @param {*} convId
    55	   * @returns {string|null}
    56	   */
    57	  function _normalizeConvId(convId) {
    58	    if (typeof convId !== 'string') return null;
    59	    if (convId.length === 0) return null;
    60	    return convId;
    61	  }
    62	
    63	  /**
    64	   * Move the convId to the head of the LRU list (MRU position). Idempotent on
    65	   * missing. Mutates envelope.lru in place.
    66	   * @param {object} envelope
    67	   * @param {string} convId
    68	   */
    69	  function _touchLru(envelope, convId) {
    70	    if (!isValidEnvelope(envelope)) return;
    71	    var key = _normalizeConvId(convId);
    72	    if (key === null) return;
    73	    var idx = envelope.lru.indexOf(key);
    74	    if (idx !== -1) envelope.lru.splice(idx, 1);
    75	    envelope.lru.unshift(key);
    76	  }
    77	
    78	  /**
    79	   * Drop entries beyond `cap` from the tail (LRU end). Idempotent. Also reaps
    80	   * any byConv entries not present in the lru array (defense vs corruption).
    81	   * Mutates envelope in place.
    82	   * @param {object} envelope
    83	   * @param {number} cap
    84	   */
    85	  function _enforceLruCap(envelope, cap) {
    86	    if (!isValidEnvelope(envelope)) return;
    87	    var effectiveCap = (typeof cap === 'number' && cap >= 0) ? cap : DEFAULT_CAP;
    88	    while (envelope.lru.length > effectiveCap) {
    89	      var tailKey = envelope.lru.pop();
    90	      if (tailKey) delete envelope.byConv[tailKey];
    91	    }
    92	    var lruSet = {};
    93	    for (var i = 0; i < envelope.lru.length; i++) lruSet[envelope.lru[i]] = true;
    94	    var byConvKeys = Object.keys(envelope.byConv);
    95	    for (var j = 0; j < byConvKeys.length; j++) {
    96	      if (!lruSet[byConvKeys[j]]) delete envelope.byConv[byConvKeys[j]];
    97	    }
    98	  }
    99	
   100	  /**
   101	   * Validate a message payload. Rejects unknown role or kind values, missing
   102	   * content, or non-numeric timestamp.
   103	   * @param {*} msg
   104	   * @returns {boolean}
   105	   */
   106	  function _isValidMessage(msg) {
   107	    if (!msg || typeof msg !== 'object') return false;
   108	    if (typeof msg.role !== 'string' || !ALLOWED_ROLES[msg.role]) return false;
   109	    if (typeof msg.content !== 'string') return false;
   110	    if (typeof msg.timestamp !== 'number' || !Number.isFinite(msg.timestamp)) return false;
   111	    if (typeof msg.kind !== 'string' || !ALLOWED_KINDS[msg.kind]) return false;
   112	    return true;
   113	  }
   114	
   115	  /**
   116	   * Append a message to the per-convId log. Lazy-creates the per-convId log on
   117	   * first call. Updates lastWriteAt + LRU. Enforces cap. Mutates envelope in
   118	   * place.
   119	   * @param {object} envelope
   120	   * @param {string} convId
   121	   * @param {{role:string, content:string, timestamp:number, kind:string}} msg
   122	   * @returns {boolean} true on success, false on invalid input
   123	   */
   124	  function appendMessage(envelope, convId, msg) {
   125	    if (!isValidEnvelope(envelope)) return false;
   126	    var key = _normalizeConvId(convId);
   127	    if (key === null) return false;
   128	    if (!_isValidMessage(msg)) return false;
   129	    var log = envelope.byConv[key];
   130	    var now = Date.now();
   131	    if (!log || typeof log !== 'object' || !Array.isArray(log.messages)) {
   132	      log = {
   133	        v: ENVELOPE_VERSION,
   134	        messages: [],
   135	        lastWriteAt: now,
   136	        createdAt: now
   137	      };
   138	      envelope.byConv[key] = log;
   139	    }
   140	    log.messages.push({
   141	      role: msg.role,
   142	      content: msg.content,
   143	      timestamp: msg.timestamp,
   144	      kind: msg.kind
   145	    });
   146	    log.lastWriteAt = now;
   147	    _touchLru(envelope, key);
   148	    _enforceLruCap(envelope, DEFAULT_CAP);
   149	    return true;
   150	  }
   151	
   152	  /**
   153	   * Peek-only fetch. NO mutation. Returns an empty array on missing.
   154	   * @param {object} envelope
   155	   * @param {string} convId
   156	   * @returns {Array}
   157	   */
   158	  function getMessages(envelope, convId) {
   159	    if (!isValidEnvelope(envelope)) return [];
   160	    var key = _normalizeConvId(convId);
   161	    if (key === null) return [];
   162	    var log = envelope.byConv[key];
   163	    if (!log || !Array.isArray(log.messages)) return [];
   164	    var out = [];
   165	    for (var i = 0; i < log.messages.length; i++) {
   166	      var m = log.messages[i];
   167	      out.push({
   168	        role: m.role,
   169	        content: m.content,
   170	        timestamp: m.timestamp,
   171	        kind: m.kind
   172	      });
   173	    }
   174	    return out;
   175	  }
   176	
   177	  /**
   178	   * Remove the conversation log entry from byConv + the lru order. Idempotent
   179	   * on missing. Mutates envelope in place.
   180	   * @param {object} envelope
   181	   * @param {string} convId
   182	   */
   183	  function dropConversationMessages(envelope, convId) {

   185	    var key = _normalizeConvId(convId);
   186	    if (key === null) return;
   187	    delete envelope.byConv[key];
   188	    var idx = envelope.lru.indexOf(key);
   189	    if (idx !== -1) envelope.lru.splice(idx, 1);
   190	  }

   192	  /**

   196	   * synchronous timer cancel + immediate callback fire. flushAll() iterates
   197	   * over all pending convIds + flushes each. cancel(convId) clears the timer

   205	   * @returns {{schedule:function, flush:function, flushAll:function, cancel:function, _hasPending:function}}
   206	   */

   215	    var _clearTimeoutFn = (typeof opts.clearTimeoutFn === 'function')

   217	      : function (id) { return clearTimeout(id); };

For modern AI flow, completion fires from extension/ai/agent-loop.js:1431 (notifySidepanel inside finalizeSession), not background.js.

   158	  implication: the sidepanel handler at L2376 ("background.js fsbBroadcastAutomationLifecycle includes tabId on every automationComplete payload (verified -- session.tabId is persisted at session creation)") is FACTUALLY WRONG -- no broadcast site in either background.js or agent-loop.js includes `tabId`. The `request.tabId === 'number'` check always falls through to `_activeTabIdSnapshot`. This is critical for Symptom D: when session B completes while user is on Tab A, originatingTabId becomes TAB_A (wrong), and setIdleState clears Tab A's mirror instead of Tab B's. **But this isn't the primary D root cause** -- a stronger bug exists in the OUTER bail check (see next entry).

   161	  checked: extension/ui/sidepanel.js:2333-2340 -- the early-return + the strict-match check
   162	  found: handler structure:
   163	    `if (!isRunning && request.sessionId !== currentSessionId) return;`  (line 2334)
   164	    `if (request.sessionId === currentSessionId) { ... process ... }`     (line 2340)
   165	  Combined effect: when session B's automationComplete arrives:
   166	    - If user is on Tab A: isRunning=true, currentSessionId=SESS_A. Outer check: !true => false => DOES NOT return. Falls through to L2340: SESS_B !== SESS_A => INNER body is skipped entirely. **No persist, no DOM render, no per-tab state update for session B.**
   167	    - If user is on Tab B (where SESS_B runs): isRunning=true, currentSessionId=SESS_B. Outer check skipped. Inner matches => processes. Works correctly.
   168	  implication: this is the **primary root cause of Symptom D**. The handler has ZERO logic for "background-tab completion" -- the per-tab _tabRunningMap entries for non-current tabs are NEVER updated by automationComplete delivery. Worse, the persistence to fsbConversationMessages also never fires for background-tab sessions, so even when the user later swaps to Tab B, hydrateChatFromConversationId reads empty for conv_B.

   174	    Event 2 (sess_A completes, user on Tab A): renderLog shows {PERSIST conv_A, COMPLETE_STATUS_MSG, RENDER_VIA_ADD_COMPLETION, PERSIST conv_A, SET_IDLE} -- **2 persists into conv_A** (one via _persistMessageToConversation L2353, one via completeStatusMessage -> addCompletionMessage -> _persistMessage L1775). Only 1 DOM render (one completeStatusMessage call).
   175	  implication: this REPRODUCES Symptom D (session B never shows in chat even after swap-back) and partially explains Symptom E (double-persist in conv_A produces 2 hydrate-replay bubbles on swap-back-then-back).
   176

   178	  checked: paths that could produce a 3rd render of the same completion for session 1
   179	  found: candidates ranked:
   180	    1. **Double-persist (proven above) + hydrate-on-swap = 2 bubbles AFTER swap.** That's only 2, not 3.
   181	    2. **Live render (1) + double-persist hydrate on swap (2 more) = 3 total** -- BUT live render is in the SAME chatMessages DOM that hydrate clears before re-rendering. So live render is wiped. Net = 2.
   182	    3. **fsbSessionLogs Tier 2 hydrate fallback** triggers when Tier 1 envelope is empty. Tier 2 renders completionMessage ONCE. If the user has Tier 1 storage cleared between dispatches (unlikely), Tier 2 would render alongside live render.
   183	    4. **Multiple sidepanel listeners** (popup.js + sidepanel.js + dashboard) each render -- but only 1 sidepanel chat surface visible at a time.
   184	    5. **Service worker restart in middle of dispatch** triggers SW-wake emit (background.js:2306) + the actual completion emit + possibly the finally block. That's plausibly 2-3 emits for one session.
   185	    6. **Per-tab map corruption from QT-93i pre-fix paths** -- if `_tabRunningMap[TAB_A]` got reassigned to SESS_B mid-flight (the bug QT-93i tried to fix), then both session A's completion AND session B's completion would have `request.sessionId === currentSessionId` true, producing 2 renders. Combined with double-persist hydrate = up to 4 bubbles.
   186	  implication: Symptom E's exact "3" count is hard to deterministically reproduce from static analysis alone. The strongest candidates are: (a) double-persist + hydrate replay (gives 2), (b) per-tab map cross-contamination from a session-A completion that landed on a tab where the user later dispatched session-B, producing reentrant renders. Need live FSB MCP log inspection to confirm 3 distinct emits vs 1 emit + 2 hydrate replays. **But regardless of the exact 3-count mechanism, the DOUBLE-PERSIST itself IS a bug** (cf7bi-02 introduced it: L2353 persists via _persistMessageToConversation, then completeStatusMessage->addCompletionMessage->_persistMessage L1775 persists AGAIN with module-scope convId which equals originatingConvId when isOriginatingActive).
   187

   189	  checked: extension/background.js:7056-7133 handleStopAutomation
   190	  found: returns `success:false, error:'Session not found'` at L7128-7132 when session is absent from BOTH activeSessions AND chrome.storage.session. The fallback restore path at L7065-7083 first tries memory, then storage. If the session naturally completed seconds ago, cleanupSession (L1860 activeSessions.delete + L1862 removePersistedSession) has wiped BOTH stores. Stop arrives and gets "Session not found".
   191	  implication: **Symptom C is a CONSEQUENCE of Symptom D, not an independent bug.** The chain: session B naturally completes -> backend cleanup removes session -> UI never received the completion (Symptom D) -> Tab B's per-tab entry stays isRunning=true -> sendBtn stays disabled, stopBtn stays visible -> user clicks Stop -> background returns "Session not found" because it's already gone. The error message is misleading (should distinguish "stale stop on already-completed session" from "never existed"), but the underlying race is benign -- backend already did the right thing. Fix is cosmetic: differentiate the error case.
   192	
   193	- timestamp: 2026-06-08 (state-emitter sanity check)
   194	  checked: SessionStateEmitter usage and registration sites
   195	  found: SessionStateEmitter class defined in extension/ai/state-emitter.js but **NEVER INSTANTIATED** anywhere in the extension. createSessionHooks in background.js:1908 registers safetyHook, progressHook (tool overlay), permissionHook -- but NOT createCompletionProgressHook or createIterationProgressHook. So `sessionStateEvent` / `iteration_complete` / `session_ended` payloads are NEVER actually emitted to chrome.runtime in the live extension. The sidepanel handler at L2522-2576 is effectively dead code for the modern flow.
   196	  implication: rules out hypothesis H-E1 (iteration_complete + session_ended both firing alongside automationComplete). Only ONE channel actually fires for the modern AI loop: `notifySidepanel -> fsbBroadcastAutomationLifecycle -> chrome.runtime.sendMessage(automationComplete)`.
   197	
   198	## Eliminated
   199	
   200	- hypothesis: H-D1 (Session 2's request.conversationId arrives null/undefined; _persistMessageToConversation silently no-ops)
   201	  evidence: read of sidepanel.js L2350 shows `originatingConvId = request.conversationId || conversationId`. agent-loop.js notifySidepanel includes `conversationId: sess.conversationId || null`. handleStartAutomation persists conversationId on session creation. So session B's broadcast DOES have a valid convId. But the bug is upstream: the OUTER guard at L2334 drops the entire handler invocation before reaching the persist call.
   202	  timestamp: 2026-06-08 (after handler read)
   203	
   204	- hypothesis: H-E1 (Three SessionStateEmitter events fire: automationComplete + iteration_complete + session_ended)
   205	  evidence: SessionStateEmitter is NEVER instantiated. createSessionHooks does not register createCompletionProgressHook or createIterationProgressHook. The `case 'sessionStateEvent'` handler in sidepanel.js is effectively dead for the modern AI flow. Only one channel fires per terminal: notifySidepanel -> sendMessage(automationComplete).
   206	  timestamp: 2026-06-08
   207	
   208	- hypothesis: H-E2 (cf9eea5b removed implicit dedup -- request.conversationId per-message stream now allows duplicate persists)
   209	  evidence: PARTIALLY confirmed. The double-persist DOES exist (sidepanel.js L2353 _persistMessageToConversation, then L2362 completeStatusMessage -> addCompletionMessage -> _persistMessage L1775). But this doesn't explain a 3x DOM render in the SAME session -- only a 2x persistence which produces 2x bubbles on hydrate-replay. The 3rd bubble likely comes from interaction between live render + hydrate after a tab swap (which clears chatMessages, then replays both persisted copies).
   210	  timestamp: 2026-06-08
   211	
   212	## Resolution
   213	
   214	root_cause: |
   215	  THREE coupled symptoms, ONE primary root cause:
   216	
   217	  PRIMARY (Symptom D + chain-effect on Symptom C):
   218	    sidepanel.js automationComplete handler at L2333-2429 has NO PATH for
   219	    "completion arrives for a non-currentSessionId (background-tab session)".
   220	    The outer bail at L2334 + the strict `if (request.sessionId === currentSessionId)`
   221	    at L2340 together drop EVERYTHING for background-tab completions:
   222	    no per-tab map update, no persistence, no render. Session 2's UI is
   223	    permanently stuck because nothing ever flips its _tabRunningMap entry
   224	    back to isRunning:false and nothing persists its completion bubble.
   225	
   226	  SECONDARY (contributes to Symptom E):
   227	    The active-tab path at L2360-2371 has a DOUBLE-PERSIST bug:
   228	    L2353 calls _persistMessageToConversation against originatingConvId,
   229	    THEN L2362 calls completeStatusMessage which calls addCompletionMessage
   230	    which calls _persistMessage (L1775) against module-scope conversationId.
   231	    When isOriginatingActive===true these are the SAME conv, so the completion
   232	    bubble is persisted TWICE into fsbConversationMessages. Hydrate-on-swap
   233	    replays both copies.
   234	
   235	  TERTIARY (cosmetic, contributes to Symptom C error message):
   236	    handleStopAutomation at background.js:7128 returns "Session not found"
   237	    indistinguishably for "session never existed" vs "session naturally
   238	    completed and was cleaned up between UI-state and stop-click". The
   239	    underlying race is benign (backend already did the right thing), but
   240	    the error toast is misleading.
   241	
   242	  CONTRIBUTING ARCHITECTURAL ISSUE:
   243	    Neither background.js nor agent-loop.js includes `tabId` in any
   244	    automationComplete payload, despite sidepanel.js L2376 claiming
   245	    they do. The fallback to `_activeTabIdSnapshot` works only when the
   246	    active tab IS the originating tab; otherwise routing is wrong.
   247	
   248	fix: |
   249	  See proposed_fix per symptom below.
   250	
   251	verification: (empty -- diagnose only, no fix applied)
   252	
   253	files_changed: []
   254	
   255	## Reasoning checkpoint
   256	
   257	- Three symptoms, likely 1-3 root causes. The fact that E (3x dupe)
   258	  and D (0x for session 2) happened in the same test session is loud:
   259	  "first session got 3 copies, second got 0" almost looks like a
   260	  fan-out where the broadcast TARGETING is wrong -- 3 emits all land

    41

The pre-fix outer bail at L2334 + strict `if (request.sessionId === currentSessionId)` at L2340 together dropped EVERYTHING for background-tab completions: no persistence, no per-tab map update, no render. After the fix:
    43

    46	- `setIdleState(originatingTabId)` fires UNCONDITIONALLY -- background tab's per-tab entry flips isRunning:false.
    47	- DOM render still gated on `isOriginatingActive`.

    50	Recon-suggestion IIFE preserved verbatim.
    51

    53	
    54	Threaded `tabId` through all 13 automationComplete broadcast sites:
    55

    64	
    65	- Part 1 (9 PASS): structural sanity -- `sessionKnown` variable, `_tabRunningMap.values()` scan, `_persistMessageToConversation`, `_renderCompletionDomOnly`, NO `completeStatusMessage` call, `isOriginatingActive` gate.
    66	- Part 2 (11 PASS): D-FIX scenario -- Tab B completes while user on Tab A; exactly one persist to conv_B (not conv_A); Tab B entry flips; Tab A entry untouched; module-scope mirrors stay attached to A; sendBtn stays disabled.
    67	- Part 3 (12 PASS): E-FIX scenario -- Tab A completes with currentStatusMessage non-null; EXACTLY one persist to conv_A; NO completeStatusMessage call; NO addCompletionMessage call; one render; manual `remove()` fires; setIdleState called with tabId=100; module-scope mirrors flip to idle.

    71	
    72	### T4 (7889e3df) -- C-FIX (cosmetic polish)
    73	
    74	`background.js handleStopAutomation`: Tier 3 lookup of `chrome.storage.local.get(['fsbSessionLogs'])` when Tier 1 (activeSessions) + Tier 2 (chrome.storage.session) both miss. If sessionId is in fsbSessionLogs, returns `{ success:false, alreadyEnded:true, error:'Already completed' }`.
    75	
    76	`sidepanel.js stopAutomation`: response handler branches on `response.alreadyEnded`. On true: completes loader DOM with 'Already completed' system message (the system-type branch routes to `addMessage`, NOT `addCompletionMessage`, so no double-persist hazard), sets idle, clears state, logs forensics. On false: keeps existing error toast.
    77

    79	
    80	Per-tab mirror of `currentStatusMessage` + `currentActionGroup` (planner audit verified they have identical lifecycle):
    81	
    82	- New module state in `sidepanel.js`: `_tabStatusIntentMap = new Map()` keyed by tabId; three helpers `_persistTabStatusIntent / _restoreTabStatusIntent / _clearTabStatusIntent`.
    83	- `chrome.tabs.onActivated`: persist OUTGOING tab intent (pre-reassignment `_activeTabIdSnapshot`) BEFORE the swap; restore INCOMING tab intent AFTER the swap.
    84	- `setIdleState`: clears the intent entry on both active-tab and background-tab paths.
    85	- `case 'automationComplete'` if-branch: clears intent entry after manual loader cleanup.

    90	
    91	Re-adds the panel auto-collapse behavior that was reverted in 09576615, but uses the Chrome 141+ `chrome.sidePanel.close({windowId})` API (a separate API from the broken `setOptions({tabId, enabled:false})` path).
    92	
    93	- `background.js`: new `chrome.tabs.onActivated.addListener` immediately after `findActiveAutomationSessionForTab`. Feature-detects `typeof chrome.sidePanel.close === 'function'` (pre-141 silently skips). Per-window has-any-working-tab gate: `chrome.tabs.query({windowId})` enumerates all tabs in the activated window, filters via `findActiveAutomationSessionForTab`. If ANY tab is working, panel stays visible. Otherwise: `chrome.sidePanel.close({windowId})`, errors swallowed as warnings.
