     1	'use strict';
     2	
     3	/**
     4	 * QT-93i (tab-scoping redo) regression smoke --
     5	 *
     6	 * Exercises the two QT-93i code paths against real-runtime mocks:
     7	 *
     8	 *   Part 1 (QT-93i-01 panel-visibility per-tab)
     9	 *     1.1 chrome.tabs.onActivated -> sidePanel.setOptions(enabled:false) for non-working tab
    10	 *     1.2 chrome.tabs.onActivated -> sidePanel.setOptions(enabled:true, path) for working tab
    11	 *     1.3 No tabId in activeInfo -> early return; no setOptions call
    12	 *     1.4 chrome.sidePanel undefined -> graceful (no throw)
    13	 *
    14	 *   Part 2 (QT-93i-01 chrome.action.onClicked safe-gesture pattern)
    15	 *     2.1 setOptionsPromise + openPromise BOTH created BEFORE any await
    16	 *     2.2 open is awaited FIRST (gesture-critical); setOptions awaited LAST
    17	 *     2.3 open failure -> falls through to chrome.windows.create popup fallback
    18	 *     2.4 setOptions failure post-open does NOT trigger popup fallback
    19	 *
    20	 *   Part 3 (QT-93i-02 per-tab isRunning map)
    21	 *     3.1 _tabRunningMap + _activeTabIdSnapshot + _getTabRunningEntry exist
    22	 *     3.2 setRunningState(tabA, sessionA) records the per-tab entry
    23	 *     3.3 setRunningState on non-active tab does NOT mutate active tab UI
    24	 *     3.4 setIdleState(tabA) clears tabA's entry without touching active-tab state
    25	 *     3.5 getCurrentTabRunningState() returns the active tab's snapshot
    26	 *
    27	 *   Part 4 (QT-93i-02 chrome.tabs.onActivated re-sync + completion routing)
    28	 *     4.1 chrome.tabs.onActivated handler re-resolves _activeTabIdSnapshot
    29	 *     4.2 Switching from working tab A to idle tab B leaves tab A's running entry intact
    30	 *     4.3 automationComplete with request.tabId=X calls setIdleState(X) (per-tab routing)
    31	 *     4.4 session_ended with request.tabId=X resolves the entry before flipping idle
    32	 *
    33	 * Discipline: extract listener / handler / case bodies via brace-counting and
    34	 * eval into sandboxed Functions (Rule 3 / CLAUDE.md MEMORY). NO static-text
    35	 * grep for presence -- every assertion exercises real code paths against
    36	 * mocked chrome.* APIs.
    37	 *
    38	 * Run: node tests/sidepanel-tab-scoping-fix-redo-smoke.test.js
    39	 *
    40	 * ASCII only. No emojis.
    41	 */
    42	
    43	const path = require('path');
    44	const fs = require('fs');
    45	
    46	let passed = 0;
    47	let failed = 0;
    48	function ok(cond, msg) {
    49	  if (cond) { passed++; console.log('  PASS:', msg); }
    50	  else { failed++; console.error('  FAIL:', msg); }
    51	}
    52	
    53	const bgSrc = fs.readFileSync(path.resolve(__dirname, '../extension/background.js'), 'utf8');
    54	const spSrc = fs.readFileSync(path.resolve(__dirname, '../extension/ui/sidepanel.js'), 'utf8');
    55	
    56	console.log('\n--- QT-93i tab-scoping redo smoke ---');
    57	
    58	// --- Brace-walking extractor (matches sidepanel-mcpvisualsession-listener.test.js pattern) ---
    59	function extractAfterAnchor(src, anchor) {
    60	  var startIdx = src.indexOf(anchor);
    61	  if (startIdx === -1) return null;
    62	  var braceIdx = src.indexOf('{', startIdx);
    63	  if (braceIdx === -1) return null;
    64	  var depth = 1;
    65	  var i = braceIdx + 1;
    66	  while (i < src.length && depth > 0) {
    67	    var c = src[i];
    68	    if (c === '{') depth++;
    69	    else if (c === '}') depth--;
    70	    if (depth === 0) break;
    71	    i++;
    72	  }
    73	  if (depth !== 0) return null;
    74	  return src.slice(braceIdx + 1, i);
    75	}
    76	
    77	// =====================================================================
    78	// PART 1 -- RE-ADDED for Chrome 141+ sidePanel.close auto-collapse (A-FIX)
    79	// =====================================================================
    80	//
    81	// Updated for QT-uof-6 (.planning/debug/cluster1-routing.md Cluster 2
    82	// leftover items). The chrome.tabs.onActivated listener is RE-ADDED but
    83	// uses the Chrome 141+ chrome.sidePanel.close({windowId}) API -- a
    84	// SEPARATE API from setOptions({tabId, enabled:false}) -- with a
    85	// feature-detect (typeof chrome.sidePanel.close === 'function') so
    86	// pre-141 installs degrade silently. The original prohibition on
    87	// setOptions enabled:false (qt93i-regression.md) STILL stands; only
    88	// the new close() API is in scope.
    89	//
    90	// Per-window has-any-working-tab gate: enumerate chrome.tabs.query({windowId})
    91	// and filter via findActiveAutomationSessionForTab so a user switching from
    92	// a working Tab A to a non-working Tab B in the SAME window does NOT lose
    93	// Tab A's panel.
    94	console.log('\nPart 1 -- chrome.tabs.onActivated listener RE-ADDED for Chrome 141+ sidePanel.close auto-collapse:');
    95	ok(bgSrc.indexOf('chrome.tabs.onActivated.addListener') !== -1,
    96	   'Part 1 -- chrome.tabs.onActivated listener PRESENT in background.js (A-FIX cluster1-routing.md)');
    97	ok(bgSrc.indexOf('chrome.sidePanel.close') !== -1,
    98	   'Part 1 -- chrome.sidePanel.close call present (Chrome 141+ auto-collapse)');
    99	ok(bgSrc.indexOf('findActiveAutomationSessionForTab') !== -1,
   100	   'Part 1 -- per-window has-any-working-tab gate via findActiveAutomationSessionForTab');
   101	
   102	runPart2();
   103	
   104	// =====================================================================
   105	// PART 2 -- chrome.action.onClicked safe-gesture pattern (no await before open)
   106	// =====================================================================
   107	
   108	function runPart2() {
   109	  console.log('\nPart 2 -- chrome.action.onClicked safe-gesture:');
   110	
   111	  var onClickedAnchor = 'chrome.action.onClicked.addListener(async (tab) => {';
   112	  var onClickedBody = extractAfterAnchor(bgSrc, onClickedAnchor);
   113	  ok(onClickedBody !== null && /QT-93i-01/.test(onClickedBody),
   114	     'Part 2.0 -- chrome.action.onClicked body extractable + carries QT-93i-01 marker');

  2219	function enforceMapLimit(map, maxSize) {
  2220	  if (map.size <= maxSize) return;
  2221	  const excess = map.size - maxSize;
  2222	  const iter = map.keys();
  2223	  for (let i = 0; i < excess; i++) {
  2224	    const key = iter.next().value;
  2225	    map.delete(key);
  2226	  }
  2227	}

   520	 * @param {string} reason - The reason string (e.g. 'complete', 'stopped', 'error', 'timeout', ...)
   521	 */
   522	async function endSessionOverlays(session, reason) {
   523	  const tabId = session.tabId || session.originalTabId;
   524	  await sendSessionStatus(tabId, { phase: 'ended', reason });
   525	
   526	  if (session.previousTabId && session.previousTabId !== tabId) {
   527	    await sendSessionStatus(session.previousTabId, { phase: 'ended', reason: 'tab_switch' });
   528	    session.previousTabId = null;
   529	  }
   530	}
   531	
   532	/**
   533	 * Send session status to content script for visual feedback (viewport glow + progress overlay).
   534	 * Targets the main frame only (frameId: 0) to avoid iframe interference.
   535	 * Retries once with content-script re-injection on failure.
   536	 * @param {number} tabId - Target tab ID
   537	 * @param {Object} statusData - Status fields: phase, taskName, iteration, maxIterations, reason, animatedHighlights
   538	 */
   539	async function sendSessionStatus(tabId, statusData) {
   540	  // Build proper overlayState object that content script expects (messaging.js:1110)
   541	  // Content script reads request.overlayState, not flat fields
   542	  const session = null; // session context is optional for buildOverlayState
   543	  const overlayState = (typeof FSBOverlayStateUtils !== 'undefined' && FSBOverlayStateUtils.buildOverlayState)
   544	    ? FSBOverlayStateUtils.buildOverlayState(statusData, session)
   545	    : statusData; // fallback: send raw data if overlay-state.js failed to load
   546	  const payload = { action: 'sessionStatus', overlayState };
   547	  try {
   548	    await chrome.tabs.sendMessage(tabId, payload, { frameId: 0 });
   549	  } catch (firstErr) {
   550	    // First attempt failed -- try re-injecting the content script and retry once
   551	    try {
   552	      await ensureContentScriptInjected(tabId, 1);
   553	      await chrome.tabs.sendMessage(tabId, payload, { frameId: 0 });
   554	    } catch (retryErr) {
   555	      automationLogger.debug('sendSessionStatus delivery failed', {
   556	        tabId, phase: statusData.phase, error: retryErr.message
   557	      });
   558	    }
   559	  }
   560	}
   561	
   562	function getMcpVisualSessionManager() {
   563	  if (!mcpVisualSessionManager) {
   564	    throw new Error('MCP visual session manager unavailable');

   844	function findActiveAutomationSessionForTab(tabId) {
   845	  if (!Number.isFinite(tabId)) return null;
   846	  for (const session of activeSessions.values()) {
   847	    if (!session || session.isBackgroundAgent) continue;

   854	
   855	// QT-uof-6 (A-FIX) -- Chrome 141+ sidePanel.close auto-collapse with
   856	// per-window has-any-working-tab gate. See .planning/debug/cluster1-routing.md
   857	// Cluster 2 leftover items + memory file project_chrome_sidepanel_no_close.md
   858	// (amended in this same commit with an EXCEPTION block for the Chrome 141+
   859	// close() API).
   860	//
   861	// Pre-Chrome-141: silently skip (feature-detect via typeof). The original
   862	// memory prohibition on setOptions({tabId, enabled:false}) STILL stands --
   863	// this listener uses chrome.sidePanel.close({windowId}), which is a
   864	// SEPARATE API added in Chrome 141+ that operates per-window and DOES
   865	// close the panel reliably even when the manifest declares
   866	// side_panel.default_path.
   867	//
   868	// Per-window gate: if ANY tab in the activated tab's window has an active
   869	// automation session (e.g., user switches from working Tab A to non-working
   870	// Tab B in the SAME window), DO NOT close -- Tab A's panel must stay
   871	// visible. Only close when NO tab in the window has an active session.
   872	chrome.tabs.onActivated.addListener(async function (activeInfo) {
   873	  try {
   874	    if (typeof chrome.sidePanel === 'undefined') return;
   875	    if (typeof chrome.sidePanel.close !== 'function') return; // pre-Chrome-141
   876	
   877	    var activatedTabId = activeInfo && activeInfo.tabId;
   878	    var activatedWindowId = activeInfo && activeInfo.windowId;
   879	    if (typeof activatedTabId !== 'number' || typeof activatedWindowId !== 'number') return;
   880	
   881	    // Per-window has-any-working-tab gate.
   882	    var tabsInWindow = await chrome.tabs.query({ windowId: activatedWindowId });
   883	    var anyWorking = false;
   884	    for (var i = 0; i < tabsInWindow.length; i++) {
   885	      var t = tabsInWindow[i];
   886	      if (t && typeof t.id === 'number' && findActiveAutomationSessionForTab(t.id)) {
   887	        anyWorking = true;
   888	        break;
   889	      }
   890	    }
   891	    if (anyWorking) return; // keep panel visible -- some tab in this window still working
   892	
   893	    // No working tab in this window -- close the panel.
   894	    try {
   895	      await chrome.sidePanel.close({ windowId: activatedWindowId });
   896	    } catch (closeErr) {
   897	      console.warn('[FSB] chrome.sidePanel.close failed (non-fatal)', closeErr && closeErr.message);
   898	    }
   899	  } catch (outerErr) {
   900	    console.warn('[FSB] chrome.tabs.onActivated A-FIX handler error', outerErr && outerErr.message);
   901	  }
   902	});

codex
A second plausible runtime-only gap is emerging: the sidepanel state is module-scope inside a sidepanel document, but `background.js` calls `chrome.sidePanel.setOptions({ tabId })` and `open({ tabId })` for every `startAutomation`. If Chrome creates or retains more than one sidepanel document across tab-scoped opens, `runtime.sendMessage` can fan out to multiple sidepanel listeners, while each document has a different `_tabRunningMap`. That would neatly produce “one handler test passes, runtime still duplicates/misses.”

codex
**Root Causes**

D-PERSIST: Task B is still dropped because `sess_B` is never reliably recorded in the live sidepanel document’s `_tabRunningMap`: `handleStartAutomation` reopens/rekeys the tab-scoped sidepanel before it sends the start response, while the only code that records the returned session is the sidepanel callback, and boot recovery incorrectly maps the first global active session to whatever tab is active. Evidence: [background.js:6493](/Users/lakshmanturlapati/Desktop/FSB/automation/extension/background.js:6493), [background.js:6809](/Users/lakshmanturlapati/Desktop/FSB/automation/extension/background.js:6809), [sidepanel.js:1286](/Users/lakshmanturlapati/Desktop/FSB/automation/extension/ui/sidepanel.js:1286), [sidepanel.js:1051](/Users/lakshmanturlapati/Desktop/FSB/automation/extension/ui/sidepanel.js:1051), [background.js:5390](/Users/lakshmanturlapati/Desktop/FSB/automation/extension/background.js:5390), [sidepanel.js:2437](/Users/lakshmanturlapati/Desktop/FSB/automation/extension/ui/sidepanel.js:2437).

E-PERSIST: Task A gets 2x because a second sidepanel document/listener can also believe `sess_A` is known and append the same completion, and `automationComplete` persistence is append-only with no `sessionId`/terminal dedupe. Evidence: [sidepanel.js:2466](/Users/lakshmanturlapati/Desktop/FSB/automation/extension/ui/sidepanel.js:2466), [sidepanel-message-log.js:140](/Users/lakshmanturlapati/Desktop/FSB/automation/extension/ui/sidepanel-message-log.js:140), [sidepanel.js:2265](/Users/lakshmanturlapati/Desktop/FSB/automation/extension/ui/sidepanel.js:2265), [background.js:6493](/Users/lakshmanturlapati/Desktop/FSB/automation/extension/background.js:6493).

**Why The Test Passes**

`tests/sidepanel-background-tab-completion.test.js` only evals one extracted handler body with an ideal injected `_tabRunningMap` containing both `sess_A` and `sess_B`. It does not exercise `startAutomation`, `chrome.sidePanel.open`, sidepanel document reload/rekey behavior, boot `getStatus`, lost sender callbacks, multiple sidepanel contexts, or the 200ms debounced storage flush. So it proves the patched handler works if the correct session map already exists in that one document; it does not reproduce the runtime state that loses or misassigns that map.

**Proposed Fix Shape**

1. Do not reopen/rekey the sidepanel from `handleStartAutomation` when the sender is already `ui/sidepanel.html`; or move that open after `sendResponse`.
2. Make `getStatus` tab-scoped. Sidepanel should ask for status for the active tab, not apply `sessionIds[0]` globally.
3. Move terminal completion persistence to background/finalization, keyed by `conversationId`, before broadcast. Sidepanel rendering should not be the durable write path.
4. Add terminal dedupe keyed by `sessionId` to the persisted message schema and to the live `automationComplete` handler before append/render.
5. Add a regression test with two simulated sidepanel documents plus boot `getStatus` and a start response lost/reordered around `sidePanel.open`.

**Load-Bearing**

`state-emitter.js` and `session_ended` are not the duplicate source in this flow; `createSessionHooks()` does not register completion/iteration emitter hooks. MCP bridge also only resolves its own promise. The duplicate source is sidepanel context fanout plus non-idempotent persistence.

**Root Causes**

D-PERSIST: Task B is still dropped because `sess_B` is never reliably recorded in the live sidepanel document’s `_tabRunningMap`: `handleStartAutomation` reopens/rekeys the tab-scoped sidepanel before it sends the start response, while the only code that records the returned session is the sidepanel callback, and boot recovery incorrectly maps the first global active session to whatever tab is active. Evidence: [background.js:6493](/Users/lakshmanturlapati/Desktop/FSB/automation/extension/background.js:6493), [background.js:6809](/Users/lakshmanturlapati/Desktop/FSB/automation/extension/background.js:6809), [sidepanel.js:1286](/Users/lakshmanturlapati/Desktop/FSB/automation/extension/ui/sidepanel.js:1286), [sidepanel.js:1051](/Users/lakshmanturlapati/Desktop/FSB/automation/extension/ui/sidepanel.js:1051), [background.js:5390](/Users/lakshmanturlapati/Desktop/FSB/automation/extension/background.js:5390), [sidepanel.js:2437](/Users/lakshmanturlapati/Desktop/FSB/automation/extension/ui/sidepanel.js:2437).

E-PERSIST: Task A gets 2x because a second sidepanel document/listener can also believe `sess_A` is known and append the same completion, and `automationComplete` persistence is append-only with no `sessionId`/terminal dedupe. Evidence: [sidepanel.js:2466](/Users/lakshmanturlapati/Desktop/FSB/automation/extension/ui/sidepanel.js:2466), [sidepanel-message-log.js:140](/Users/lakshmanturlapati/Desktop/FSB/automation/extension/ui/sidepanel-message-log.js:140), [sidepanel.js:2265](/Users/lakshmanturlapati/Desktop/FSB/automation/extension/ui/sidepanel.js:2265), [background.js:6493](/Users/lakshmanturlapati/Desktop/FSB/automation/extension/background.js:6493).

**Why The Test Passes**

`tests/sidepanel-background-tab-completion.test.js` only evals one extracted handler body with an ideal injected `_tabRunningMap` containing both `sess_A` and `sess_B`. It does not exercise `startAutomation`, `chrome.sidePanel.open`, sidepanel document reload/rekey behavior, boot `getStatus`, lost sender callbacks, multiple sidepanel contexts, or the 200ms debounced storage flush. So it proves the patched handler works if the correct session map already exists in that one document; it does not reproduce the runtime state that loses or misassigns that map.

**Proposed Fix Shape**

1. Do not reopen/rekey the sidepanel from `handleStartAutomation` when the sender is already `ui/sidepanel.html`; or move that open after `sendResponse`.
2. Make `getStatus` tab-scoped. Sidepanel should ask for status for the active tab, not apply `sessionIds[0]` globally.
3. Move terminal completion persistence to background/finalization, keyed by `conversationId`, before broadcast. Sidepanel rendering should not be the durable write path.
4. Add terminal dedupe keyed by `sessionId` to the persisted message schema and to the live `automationComplete` handler before append/render.
5. Add a regression test with two simulated sidepanel documents plus boot `getStatus` and a start response lost/reordered around `sidePanel.open`.

**Load-Bearing**

`state-emitter.js` and `session_ended` are not the duplicate source in this flow; `createSessionHooks()` does not register completion/iteration emitter hooks. MCP bridge also only resolves its own promise. The duplicate source is sidepanel context fanout plus non-idempotent persistence.
